candidates.japanese: ゴムをすると確かに性感が落ちるので、出来れば生でしたいです。
candidates.english: The sensitivity certainly drops if I use a condom, so if possible I'd like to do it bareback.

candidates.japanese: よく聞いたら、ネタじゃなくてマジボケだったみたいです。
candidates.english: When I asked afterwards it seems he hadn't said that as a joke.

candidates.japanese: フェアにやろうとは申しませんけど、無思慮な行動はおやめ頂きたいですわ。
candidates.english: I will not say play fair but I would wish that you refrain from indiscreet acts.

candidates.japanese: 疲れたから、コーヒーカップに乗ってゆっくりしたいです。
candidates.english: I'm tired. I just want to go on the teacup ride and take it easy.

candidates.japanese: 熱いココアが飲みたいですね。
candidates.english: I'd like to have some hot chocolate.

candidates.japanese: 朝食に何を食べたいですか。
candidates.english: What do you want for breakfast?

candidates.japanese: 朝食に何をとりたいですか。
candidates.english: What do you want for breakfast?

candidates.japanese: 朝食にはゆで卵をとりたいですか。
candidates.english: Do you want a boiled egg for breakfast?

candidates.japanese: オバマさんが大阪から東京に転入したいです。
candidates.english: Mr. Obama wants to move to Tokyo from Osaka.

candidates.japanese: 大きくなったら何になりたいですか。
candidates.english: What do you want to be when you grow up?

candidates.japanese: 政治家になりたいです。
candidates.english: I want to be a statesman.

candidates.japanese: 是非そうしたいですね。
candidates.english: I'd like that a lot.

candidates.japanese: 診察していただきたいです。
candidates.english: I'd like to see the doctor.

candidates.japanese: 将来何になりたいですか？
candidates.english: What do you want to be in the future?

candidates.japanese: 将来、彼は何になりたいですか。
candidates.english: What would he like to be in the future?

candidates.japanese: 出発する前に会いたいです。
candidates.english: I would like to see you before I leave.

candidates.japanese: 次はどこへ行きたいですか。
candidates.english: Where would you like to go next?

candidates.japanese: 次の日曜日にはどこへ行きたいですか。
candidates.english: Where would you like to go next Sunday?

candidates.japanese: 私は来春大学を卒業したいです。
candidates.english: I hope to graduate from university next spring.

candidates.japanese: 私は大変気分が悪い。ゲロをはきたいです。
candidates.english: I feel very sick. I want to throw up.

candidates.japanese: 私は将来できればパイロットになりたいです。
candidates.english: I want to be a pilot in the future.

candidates.japanese: 私は自由の女神像を見たいです。
candidates.english: I'd like to see the Statue of Liberty.

candidates.japanese: 私はフライトアテンダントの職につきたいです。
candidates.english: I long for a job as a flight attendant.

candidates.japanese: 私はジョーのグループに加わりたいです。
candidates.english: I want to join Joe's group.

candidates.japanese: この山について知りたいです。
candidates.english: I want to know about this mountain.

candidates.japanese: 私はいつかまたその少女に会いたいです。
candidates.english: I want to see that girl again some day.

candidates.japanese: 私はあなたに車を洗ってもらいたいです。
candidates.english: I want you to wash the car.

candidates.japanese: それはあなたが一人になりたくないからです。
candidates.english: It's because you don't want to be alone.

candidates.japanese: 行き過ぎたものであっても、僕は自分の考えをなくしたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to lose my ideas, even though some of them are a bit extreme.

candidates.japanese: そんなに待ちたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to wait that long.

candidates.japanese: 「どうして行かないの？」「行きたくないからだよ」
candidates.english: "Why aren't you going?" "Because I don't want to."

candidates.japanese: そのことを後悔しながら余生を送りたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to spend the rest of my life regretting it.

candidates.japanese: 学校へ行きたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to go to school.

candidates.japanese: 彼女はそのことについて話したくないんだよ。
candidates.english: She doesn't want to talk about it.

candidates.japanese: 君の愚痴はもう聞きたくないよ。
candidates.english: I don't want to hear any more of your complaining.

candidates.japanese: ＯＫ！！ブラザー！！それ以上聞きたくないぜ！
candidates.english: OK!! Bro!! I don't want to hear any more!

candidates.japanese: ３０過ぎての商売替えは大変だからしたくないのですけれどね。
candidates.english: Well it's very difficult changing jobs after you're thirty so I don't really want to do it.

candidates.japanese: ぼくはやばい橋は渡りたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to do anything risky.

candidates.japanese: けったくそ悪いから、やりたくないことはやらない。
candidates.english: 'Cuz it's a giant pain in the arse, if I don't wanna do something I just won't do it.

candidates.japanese: 両親にこれ以上負担をかけたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to be any more burden to my parents.

candidates.japanese: 率直に言って私はあなたと行きたくない。
candidates.english: Frankly speaking, I don't want to go with you.

candidates.japanese: 率直に言って、私は彼と一緒に働きたくない。
candidates.english: Frankly speaking, I don't want to work with him.

candidates.japanese: 無駄な努力はしたくない。
candidates.english: I do not want to exert myself in vain.

candidates.japanese: 僕は彼のような奴を尊敬したくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to respect a man like him.

candidates.japanese: 僕はこれ以上の仕事は引き受けたくない。
candidates.english: I don't like to take on any more work.

candidates.japanese: 不平はまったくない。
candidates.english: We have nothing to complain about.

candidates.japanese: 貧乏くじを引きたくない。
candidates.english: I don't want to be left holding the bag.

candidates.japanese: 飛行機に乗り遅れたくないんだ。
candidates.english: I don't want to miss my flight.

candidates.japanese: 彼等によって私は危険がまったくないと信じる気になった。
candidates.english: They led me to believe that there was no danger.

candidates.japanese: 彼女はもう二度と私の顔を見たくないときっぱり言った。
candidates.english: She told me once and for all that she did not want to see me again.

candidates.japanese: 彼女はビルと交際したくないと思っている。
candidates.english: She doesn't like to associate with Bill.

candidates.japanese: もし間違いを見つけたら訂正してください。
candidates.english: If you see a mistake, then please correct it.

candidates.japanese: 最初着る前に洗濯してください。
candidates.english: Wash before first wearing.

candidates.japanese: 口を開けてください！
candidates.english: Open your mouth!

candidates.japanese: もっとゆっくり話してください！
candidates.english: Speak more slowly, please!

candidates.japanese: 暇だったら、手伝ってください。
candidates.english: If you are free, give me a hand.

candidates.japanese: as may be の文法解釈を教えてください。
candidates.english: Please explain the grammar of 'as may be'.

candidates.japanese: どうぞ、皆様も最後の一瞬まで粘り抜いてください。
candidates.english: Will everyone please stick with it to the last moment.

candidates.japanese: 洗濯のりでののりづけの仕方を教えてください。
candidates.english: Please tell me how to use laundry starch to starch things.

candidates.japanese: 「どいてください」「やんのか？あんちゃん」
candidates.english: "Please move aside." "Ya wanna make something of it, sonny?"

candidates.japanese: はきはきと意見を言ってください。
candidates.english: Please state your opinion crisply and clearly.

candidates.japanese: 持ち物すべてに記名してください。
candidates.english: Please put their name on everything they take with them.

candidates.japanese: お願いですからヒスを起こす前に話は最後まで聞いてください。
candidates.english: I'm begging you, before freaking out on me listen to the end of what I have to say.

candidates.japanese: プラモ作りは家でやってくださいよ。ラッカー液の臭い、充満してるじゃないですか。
candidates.english: Please do your model building at home! The room's full of the smell of lacquer.

candidates.japanese: 本当にこじらせなくて良かった。でもまだ無理はしないようにしてください。
candidates.english: Thank goodness it didn't worsen. But please don't push yourself yet.

candidates.japanese: 表彰式？表彰台？のポディウムの綴りを教えてください。
candidates.english: Awards ceremony podium? Victory stand podium? Please tell me the correct spelling.

candidates.japanese: 彼方任せにしないで、自分で責任をもってやってください。
candidates.english: Instead of leaving it to others, take responsibility yourself.

candidates.japanese: 単語は徐々に増えますので、こまめにチェックしてください。
candidates.english: The words covered are steadily increasing, so check frequently.

candidates.japanese: 詳らかに教えてください。
candidates.english: Please explain in detail.

candidates.japanese: 御社のお考えを先におっしゃってください。
candidates.english: Please let us know your company's thoughts on this matter first.

candidates.japanese: りんごを少し買ってください。
candidates.english: Please buy a few apples.

candidates.japanese: バットを長めに持ってください。
candidates.english: Please hold your bat by the end of its handle.

candidates.japanese: とろ火で時間をかけて豆を炊いてください。
candidates.english: Please simmer the beans for a while over a low heat.

candidates.japanese: お子さんが車をおりたらすぐに車を出してください。
candidates.english: Please drive off as soon as your child has left the car.

candidates.japanese: ５ページ脚注を参照してください。
candidates.english: See the footnote on page 5.

candidates.japanese: 籠の鳥に水とえさをまいにちやるようにしてください。
candidates.english: Please see that the birds in the cage get water and food every day.

candidates.japanese: 脇へ寄ってくださいませんか。
candidates.english: Would you mind standing aside?

candidates.japanese: 脇によってください。
candidates.english: Please step aside.

candidates.japanese: 録音が終わったらそれを再生して聞かせてください。
candidates.english: Please play it back for me after you've finished the recording.

candidates.japanese: 六時半に起こしてください。
candidates.english: Please wake me up at 6:30.

candidates.japanese: 連絡先をここに書いてください。
candidates.english: Please write down your contact address here.
